original_tool_input.base_case: Over the next 30-60 days the highest single-outcome probability remains a melt-up toward the 7,705 upside target (Monte Carlo P(up-first)=65%), but the structural backdrop — extreme extension (+11.2% vs 200dma, narrative cites +15.1%), sub-0.10% ERP, persistent breadth/credit divergence, and a sharply widening junk-bond spread — argues that any further upside is a final-gasp trim window rather than a durable advance. Base case: grind/churn near ATHs with an obligation to trim into strength and a deeper mean-reversion risk if credit stress resolves violently.
original_tool_input.trend_regime: 50-day SMA above 200-day SMA with price above both; trend intact but extension at cycle highs (+10.6% vs 50dma, +15.1% vs 200dma per narrative) — bullish but maturing/late-stage.
original_tool_input.valuation_bucket: Forward PE ~21.5x (20-22x cautious band, trim bias increasing); ERP ~0.0% sitting at the valuation-ceiling floor (0.0-0.5% band) — trim bias, no aggressive adds.
what_changed_today: Seventh consecutive ATH close but smallest gain of the streak (+0.13% / +9.82 pts), 35% below the streak average — momentum decelerating sharply.
RSI-14 closed at ~70, the first ATH-day close below the overbought threshold this cycle — a fresh momentum crack vs prior overbought ATH closes.
Overall Fear & Greed fell a 4th consecutive session (61->57), extending the sentiment fade flagged on 2026-06-01.
Junk bond spread spiked to ~1.45%, widest since the April tariff shock — credit divergence escalated materially vs prior session.
McClellan breadth ticked up a 2nd day (~1,060), modest improvement but still in Fear; participation watch from prior run continues unresolved.
Decision score deteriorated to 7 RED / 6 CAUTION / 3 GREEN, +1 Red vs prior session.
original_tool_input.narrative_summary: SPX printed a seventh consecutive all-time high at 7,609.78 but the move is stalling: the smallest daily gain of the streak, an intraday close in the lower half of the range, RSI slipping to ~70, and a Monte Carlo upside edge that still clears only the 65% threshold (not 70%/75%). Beneath the surface the divergences are widening — sub-0.10% ERP at the valuation ceiling, breadth and Stock Price Strength stuck in Fear, sentiment fading four sessions running, and a junk-bond spread spiking to April-shock levels. The structural bias is Late Bull / Topping, so the melt-up path is a trim obligation rather than a buy signal. Tactical posture: hold the core, be actively prepared to execute Wave 1 trims into the 7,705 upside zone, and stage re-entry capital toward the 7,449/7,428 fib zone and deeper liquidation levels.
open_questions: Will price tag the ~7,705 upside / Wave 1 trim ceiling before momentum fully exhausts, and is the trim executed cleanly on touch?
Does the ~1.45% junk-bond spread keep widening — forcing a VIX spike to match credit stress — or does it compress back toward equity complacency?
Does McClellan breadth (~1,060) continue its tentative recovery toward ~1,100 to confirm participation, or roll back into deeper Fear?
Does ERP hold the ~0.0% ceiling or tip negative, hardening the trim-only posture?

confirming_evidence: Smallest daily gain of a 7-session ATH streak (+0.13%) with weak lower-half intraday close — momentum exhaustion confirming late-stage trim posture.
RSI-14 closing ~70 (first ATH-day close below overbought) plus flat-declining MFI = bearish momentum divergence at the highs.
ERP ~0.0% at the valuation ceiling and forward PE ~21.5x both support a trim-bias / no-aggressive-add valuation stance.
Walking the upper Bollinger band for 14 sessions confirms an extended trim window rather than a fresh breakout.
Fear & Greed fading 4 consecutive sessions during new highs — classic topping warning.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Monte Carlo P(up-first)=65% clears the Late Bull threshold's lower analog and median 9 days to the 7,705 upside target favors a melt-up.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: Decelerating daily gains, RSI ~70 crack, weak intraday close, and 14-session upper-band ride signal the rally is stalling — the model cannot price these.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: Never use Monte Carlo output in isolation; interpret through structural bias and chart evidence. In Late Bull / Topping the action threshold is 70% — 65% is not actionable.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Equities at fresh ATHs and VIX ~15.77 (complacent) signal no stress in the volatility market.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: Junk-bond spread spiked to ~1.45%, widest since the April tariff shock — credit is screaming danger while equities and VIX show none; resolution historically favors VIX spiking to match credit.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: When breadth and credit diverge from price, elevate caution even if price has not broken down; sustained spread widening above recent average = escalating alarm.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Price continues making new all-time highs with trend fully intact.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: ERP ~0.0% (sub-0.10%, cycle low) means equities offer essentially no premium over risk-free yield — valuation ceiling reached even as price rises.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: ERP 0.0-0.5% = valuation ceiling, trim bias, no aggressive adds; below 0.0% = trim only.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Headline Fear & Greed still in Greed (57) and momentum components in Extreme Greed.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: Stock Price Strength stuck in Fear 8 sessions and McClellan breadth in Fear (~1,060) — headline strength masks narrow leadership, and sentiment is fading 4 days running.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: Headline strength with weak breadth indicates narrow leadership; falling sentiment near new highs is a topping warning.

original_tool_input.primary_tension: An intact, still-rising bull trend with a Monte Carlo upside edge (65% up-first, melt-up favored) is colliding with cycle-extreme exhaustion signals — decelerating gains, RSI cracking below overbought, sub-zero-cushion ERP, and a junk-bond spread blowing out to April-shock levels — making further upside a trim obligation rather than a buy.
